title: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
snippet: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

title: Fragile quiet in Lebanon as US-Iran truce leaves unanswered questions
snippet: Many Lebanese remain doubtful that the agreement could finally mean the end of the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah.

title: UK investigating reports Russian warship fired warning shots near yacht in English Channel
snippet: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

title: Russian artist and Putin critic shot dead in Poland
snippet: Robert Kuzovkov, who used the pseudonym Semyon Skrepetsky, has been known for his caricatures of politicians including Vladimir Putin.

title: Brazil convicts Jair Bolsonaro's son of pursuing US help in father's legal battle
snippet: Eduardo Bolsonaro called the conviction "baseless and senseless".

snippet: Police have arrested nine people and rescued more than 400 cats destined for slaughter, with more than 40 reunited with their owners.

title: France's oldest female detainee, 79, goes on trial for in-law's grisly murder

snippet: The incident occurred on Monday morning while the aircraft had been on a routine test mission.

snippet: The measures will see apps including TikTok and Snapchat banned for UK teens early in 2027.

snippet: Helium-3 is expensive and demand is forecast to soar, so some are planning to mine it on the moon.

snippet: Elon Musk's rocket firm has overtaken the retail and media empire in value after a surge in its share price.

title: Inflation remains at 2.8%, slightly lower than expected

title: What is El Niño and why could it mean record temperatures?

snippet: Artemis III will help test crucial systems needed for the planned Artemis IV Moon landing in 2028.
